meta_description: Polycarbonate boasts great cosmetic appearance, durability, and resistance, making it a great option for consumer products or housings, as well as medical, aerospace, and lighting applications.

sections.heading: Polycarbonate Injection Molding
sections.text: Polycarbonate Injection Molding Polycarbonate boasts great cosmetic appearance and durability Get a Quote

sections.heading: Polycarbonate boasts great cosmetic appearance and durability
sections.text: Polycarbonate boasts great cosmetic appearance and durability

sections.heading: Polycarbonate Applications
sections.text: Polycarbonate Applications PC’s outstanding dimensional stability, impact/scratch resistance, and biocompatibility make the amorphous thermoplastic a top choice in the medical, aerospace, and consumer electronic industries. Optically clear grades are popular in the lighting industry as lenses and reflectors. Other common applications include: Electronic housings Electronic handheld devices Electrical components Medical devices Optical lenses Indoor and outdoor lighting Bulletproof glass Appliances

sections.heading: Lexan 940 Offers Good Looks and Strength for Molded Parts
sections.text: Lexan 940 Offers Good Looks and Strength for Molded Parts Lexan 940 resin continues to be a staple polycarbonate (PC) used for injection molding. Read Blog arrow_right_alt Selecting the Right Plastic for Your Next Molded Part Learn the key characteristics of common injection molding materials and recommended applications. Read Design Tip arrow_right_alt Enhancing Cosmetic Appearance on Molded Parts The success of the products we design can often be affected by their visual appearance. In addition to the visual appearance, the cosmetics of a plastic part impacts the tactile feel. Quality judgments are often by how a product feels when we hold it or touch it. Read Guide arrow_right_alt Thermo...
